meta.title: The phosphorylation of ribosomal protein S6 in hamster fibroblasts infected with pseudorabies virus inactivated by ultraviolet radiation -- Kennedy et al. 65 (9): 1621 -- Journal of General Virology

abstractHtml: Infection of baby hamster fibroblasts with pseudorabies virus at high multiplicities resulted in a substantial increase in the phosphorylation of ribosomal protein S6. However, the phosphorylation was still observed with virus that had been completely inactivated by u.v. irradiation. We therefore conclude that expression of the viral genome is not required for the virus to elicit this effect.
